title: CINSULIN
description: Cinsulin is a dietary supplement formulation primarily composed of water-soluble cinnamon extract (Cinnulin PF) combined with chromium and often carnosine. It works by enhancing insulin sensitivity and potentiating insulin-mediated glucose uptake, making it a common intervention for managing blood sugar levels in pre-diabetic or metabolic syndrome contexts.

research.title: Effect of cinnamon spice on continuously monitored glycemic response in adults with prediabetes: a 4-week randomized controlled crossover trial
research.summary: Daily cinnamon supplementation significantly lowered 24-hour blood glucose concentrations and reduced glucose peaks in adults with obesity and prediabetes.

research.title: A Dietary Supplement Containing Cinnamon, Chromium and Carnosine Decreases Fasting Plasma Glucose and Increases Lean Mass in Overweight or Obese Pre-Diabetic Subjects: A Randomized, Placebo-Controlled Trial
research.summary: The combination of cinnamon, chromium, and carnosine significantly decreased fasting plasma glucose and helped increase lean body mass in overweight individuals.

research.title: Cinnamon extract lowers glucose, insulin and cholesterol in people with elevated serum glucose
research.summary: Water-soluble cinnamon extract was shown to improve glycemic indicators and lower cholesterol levels in patients with elevated serum glucose.

sentiment.summary: Users generally report high satisfaction with Cinsulin for stabilizing blood sugar levels and curbing sugar cravings, often viewing it as a safer, natural alternative to pharmaceutical glucose-lowering agents.

effects.label: HbA1c Reduction:
effects.description: Users and studies indicate it is effective at lowering markers of long-term blood sugar levels in pre-diabetic individuals.

effects.label: Synergistic Formula:
effects.description: The combination of chromium and cinnamon is noted as more effective than cinnamon alone for insulin signaling.

effects.label: Fast-Acting:
effects.description: Some users notice improved energy stability and reduced 'brain fog' after meals within the first week of use.

dosage.label: Standard Capsule:
dosage.description: Most commercial brands recommend two capsules daily, providing roughly 500mg of cinnamon extract.

dosage.label: Pre-Meal Timing:
dosage.description: Users suggest taking the supplement 15-30 minutes before high-carbohydrate meals for maximum effect.

dosage.label: Clinical Dosage:
dosage.description: Research trials often utilize 4g of raw spice equivalent or specific concentrated water-soluble extracts.

sideEffects.label: Hypoglycemia:
sideEffects.description: Potential for blood sugar to drop too low if taken in conjunction with prescription diabetes medication.

sideEffects.label: Digestive Discomfort:
sideEffects.description: Minor reports of heartburn or mild stomach upset if taken without sufficient water.

sideEffects.label: Skin Flushing:
sideEffects.description: Rarely reported, likely due to sensitivities to specific cinnamon compounds in non-water-soluble extracts.

availability.label: Retail Availability:
availability.description: Widely available as an over-the-counter supplement in major retailers and online stores.

availability.label: Extract Type:
availability.description: It is important to look for 'water-soluble' extracts (like Cinnulin PF) to avoid toxic coumarin found in whole cinnamon powder.
